data.title: U.K. to Launch a New October 2035 Gilt Via Syndication in Week Starting Sept. 1
data.description:
By Miriam Mukuru

The U.K. Debt Management Office plans to launch a new October 2035 gilt via syndication in the week starting Sept. 1, subject to demand and market conditions, it said Friday.

It will be the third syndication by the DMO in the fiscal year 2026.

"Further details of this transaction, including the appointment of the syndicate and the coupon on the new gilt, will be announced in due course," the DMO said.

Write to Miriam Mukuru at miriam.mukuru@wsj.com

data.title: Next Technology Holding Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Next Technology Holding Inc., a company specializing in AI-enabled software development services and Bitcoin acquisition, has released its Form 10-Q report for the six-month period ended June 30, 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and strategic business activities, providing insights into its operational focus and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Service Revenue: $0 million. The company did not generate any service revenue for the six-month period ended June 30, 2025, and 2024.
  • Gross Profit: $0 million. No gross profit was recorded as there was no service revenue.
  • Loss from Operations: $(705,820). Operating expenses were primarily driven by general and administrative expenses.
  • Net Income: $312.01 million. The significant increase in net income was mainly due to a fair value gain from digital assets.
  • Net Comprehensive Income Per Share, Basic and Diluted from Continuing Operation: $1.16. Reflects the substantial net income achieved during the period.

Business Highlights

  • Business Strategy: Next Technology Holding Inc. pursues two main strategies: providing AI-enabled software development services and acquiring and holding Bitcoin. The company aims to leverage its software development expertise while strategically managing its Bitcoin holdings for potential appreciation.
  • Software Development Services: The company offers AI-enabled software development services, including developing, designing, and implementing various SaaS software solutions for a diverse range of businesses, including industrial sectors.
  • Bitcoin Acquisition Strategy: The company actively acquires Bitcoin using liquid assets exceeding working capital requirements. It views Bitcoin as a trading asset, aiming to accumulate when prices are low and sell when high, without specific holding targets.
  • Operational Performance: For the six-month period ended June 30, 2025, the company reported no revenue from its software development services, indicating a focus on its Bitcoin acquisition strategy.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue monitoring market conditions to determine additional financing for Bitcoin purchases. It also considers selling Bitcoin for corporate purposes and exploring strategies to generate income from its Bitcoin holdings.
  • Segment Information: The company operates as a single reportable segment, with centralized and integrated operations. The Chief Executive Officer reviews financial information on a consolidated basis, focusing on net income as the primary performance measure.

SEC Filing: Next Technology Holding Inc. [ NXTT ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: German 2-year yields set for weekly rise as traders unwind bets on ECB cuts
data.description:
Key points:
  • BoE's hawkish cut supports euro zone short-dated yields
  • Traders return to pricing 80% chance of a March 2026 ECB cut
  • Barclays sees lower US yields after recent data
  • Italian-German spread hits fresh 15-year low

Germany’s two-year government bond yields were set for a weekly rise, as traders unwound rate-cut bets that had surged following last week's weaker-than-expected U.S. jobs data.

The Bank of England's policy decision pushed two-year gilt yields higher on Thursday, helping lift euro zone short-dated borrowing costs as well.

Analysts expect U.S. Treasury yields to decline, while euro zone yields are seen rising later this year as Germany kicks off a major fiscal spending push aimed at reviving growth.

Germany’s 10-year government bond yields (DE10YT=RR), the euro area’s benchmark, were set for a second straight weekly decline, down around 3.5 basis points (bps), and last up 0.5 bps on the day at 2.65%.

The policy-sensitive German two-year yield (DE2YT=RR) was on track for a weekly rise of 3 basis points, trading 1 bp higher on the day at 1.93%.

Market pricing for Federal Reserve rate cuts held steady at levels seen last Friday after the latest economic data, with around a 90% chance of an easing move next month and 124 basis points of cuts priced in by October 2026.

ECB euro short-term rate (ESTR) forwards last implied an 80% chance of a 25-basis-point rate cut in March 2026 (EURESTECBM5X6=ICAP), down from fully pricing it after Friday's U.S. jobs data. The depo rate is currently at 2%.

However, traders also see a good chance of a rate hike by December 2026, with the deposit rate projected at 1.9%. (EURESTECBM1X12=ICAP)

“We believe the implications of the recent economic data are generally in favour of lower (U.S.) Treasury yields,” said Anshul Pradhan, head of U.S. rates research at Barclays.

“The revised jobs data have helped reconcile the discrepancy between the apparent resilience of job gains and real gross domestic product growth having moved sharply lower amid rising tariff rates in the first half of the year.”

Markets are also watching closely for signs of who might succeed Jerome Powell as Fed Chair, with Christopher Waller emerging as a top candidate. Powell is expected to step down when his term ends in May 2026.

“Waller as next Fed Chair would reduce uncertainty about the Fed reaction function on the margin, which would also bode well for the long end,” Barclays’ Pradhan argued.

Bond prices move inversely to their yields.

UK two-year gilt yields (GB2YT=RR) were last up 2 bps at 3.90% after rising 5.5 bps the day before.

Italy’s 10-year government bond yields were up one bp at 3.47%. The yield gap between Italian and German bonds (DE10IT10=RR) – a market gauge of the risk premium investors demand to hold Italian debt – hit a fresh 15-year low at 81 bps.

Analysts argued that with the ECB easing cycle close to an end, the air for a further BTP-Bund spread compression will probably become thinner after the summer.

They said technical factors supporting BTPs could also fade. Positive ratings kicked off the tightening, but declining volatility favouring carry trades and lower supply over the summer backed the rally.

data.title: Lamar Advertising Posts Higher 2Q Profit, Revenue
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Lamar Advertising logged higher top and bottom lines in the second quarter as revenue growth accelerated, though the company warned sales are likely to slow in the back half of the year.

The outdoor advertising company on Friday posted a profit of $155 million, or $1.52 a share, compared with $137.6 million, or $1.34 a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted funds from operations were $2.22 a share, ahead of the $2.18 a share that analysts surveyed by FactSet had expected.

Revenue climbed 2.5% to $579.3 million, roughly in line with Wall Street models.

Chief Executive Sean Reilly attributed the higher top line to an acceleration in both national and local advertising sales during the quarter. "Our pacings indicate further year-over-year improvement in revenues is likely in the second half of 2025, though perhaps not to the degree that we'd anticipated entering the year," he added.

As a result, Lamar now expects adjusted funds from operations between $8.10 and $8.20 a share, down from a prior outlook of $8.13 to $8.28 a share. Analysts are looking for $8.43 a share.

Shares edged 0.5% lower, to $124.14, in premarket trading.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Emera Profit Flat in 2Q
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Emera's second-quarter profit flat as Florida utilities offset softness in Canadian market.

The Canadian energy holding company on Friday posted net income of 135 million Canadian dollars ($98.2 million), or C$0.45 a share, compared with C$129 million, or C$0.45 a share, in the comparable quarter a year ago.

Adjusted earnings were C$0.79 a share, topping analyst expectations of C$0.65 a share, according to FactSet. Emera credits the rise to largely to strong growth and favorable weather in Florida.

The higher adjusted earnings in the period came from its Florida Electric Utility segment, which rose to C$260 million from C$187 million, offsetting declines in its Canadian Electric Utilities segment which fell by more than half, to C$17 million from C$42 million.

Emera reiterated its targets of 5% to 7% annual average adjusted EPS growth through 2027 and 7% to 8% forecasted rate base growth through 2029.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: India's Manappuram Finance posts drop in first-quarter profit on higher provisions
data.description:
Indian gold-loan financier Manappuram Finance reported a drop in first-quarter profit on Friday, hurt by a jump in bad loan provisions.

The company's consolidated net profit fell 75% year-on-year to 1.38 billion rupees for the quarter ended June 30.

Manappuram approved the appointment of V. P. Nandakumar as the chairman of the company.

data.title: North American Morning Briefing: Stock Futures, Gold Prices Rise; Investors Weigh Miran Nomination
data.description:
OPENING CALL

U.S. stock futures rose Friday as investors looked toward the end of a busy week of earnings and tariff developments with confidence.

A report the U.S. slapped tariffs on gold imports was the latest to affect markets, sending gold futures upward.

Much focus was on Trump's nomination of Stephen Miran to temporarily fill a vacancy on the Federal Reserve's Board of Governors.

Capital Economics said the president made a good choice in Miran, calling his nomination "a welcome surprise."

Meanwhile, antitrust officials in the U.K. effectively cleared Boeing's plan to acquire Spirit AeroSystems Holdings, saying they won't open an in-depth investigation into the transaction.

Stocks to Watch

Expedia Group traded 18% higher after it raised its full-year outlook.

Gilead Sciences rose 2.9% after it raised its guidance and said it saw strong demand for its HIV medicine.

Pinterest shares fell 13% after earnings disappointed investors.

Trade Desk shares declined 29% after posting second-quarter earnings.

Twilio shares were up 13% after its second-quarter adjusted earnings per share beat estimates.

Watch For:

Canada Labour Force Survey for July

Today's Top Headlines/Must Reads:

- Why a Weight-Loss Pill Is Still a Big Deal — Heard on the Street

- The Race to Find a Measles Treatment as Infections Surge

- Delta and Alaska Air Square Off in Battle for Seattle

MARKET WRAPS

Forex:

The dollar fell against a basket of currencies, after President Trump said he would nominate Stephen Miran to temporarily join the Federal Reserve's board of governors.

"Miran has expressed skepticism about the Fed's independence and has argued for stronger presidential control over the Fed Board," Danske Bank said.

ING said, however, that the dollar's fall was only modest and the effect of Miran's nomination was being offset by reports that Fed Governor Christopher Waller was the favorite to replace Chair Jerome Powell.

Bonds:

Treasury yields rose slightly after Thursday's auction of 30-year notes saw weak demand.

Demand for Treasurys waned after recent weak jobs data and President Trump's decision to nominate Miran also weighed, Deutsche Bank said .

Metals:

Gold futures rose driven by a report the U.S. imposed import tariffs on gold bars. President Trump's other trade levies, as well as expectations the Fed would soon cut interest rates, also spurred the metal's rise.

Energy:

Crude prices were up slightly but were on track for sharp weekly losses on news the U.S. and Russia could meet as soon as next week, and as U.S. tariffs weighed on the global economic outlook.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Intel's CEO, Under Attack From Trump, Is Already at Odds With His Board

Intel Chief Executive Lip-Bu Tan was already at odds with some board members before President Trump jumped into the fray.

Tan and some Intel directors have disagreed in his first months in the role about questions as central as whether the company should stay in the manufacturing business or exit it entirely, according to people familiar with the matter. Recent efforts by Tan to raise new capital and acquire an artificial-intelligence company have been stalled by people on the board, they said.

Apple Got the Jump on Tariffs, Deciding Years Ago to Make iPhones in India

SRIPERUMBUDUR, India-Dozens of women wearing ID badges streamed from their rooms in a year-old dormitory for Foxconn workers and headed to a company cafeteria on a recent day for a menu of lentil-and-vegetable stew, beetroot and rice.

White buses waited outside to ferry them to a factory where the Apple contractor builds iPhones.

Chip Maker SMIC Sees Limited Impact of Trump's Tariffs

China's largest chip maker, Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corp., said the U.S. tariffs should continue to have a limited impact as demand from domestic clients is increasing sharply and overseas clients are better prepared for the headwinds.

"At least until around October this year, SMIC's production capacity will still be in short supply" as the expected hard landing of tariffs didn't happen, SMIC co-chief executive Zhao Haijun said Friday in a post-earnings call.

K-Pop Looks to China to Supercharge Growth After Strong Earnings

The K-pop industry has been on a roll with a string of solid results, but a thawing of China-South Korea relations could supercharge growth.

Hybe, the powerhouse agency behind K-pop boy band BTS, saw its second-quarter revenue rise 10% from year earlier to about $510 million, a record for the period, while its operating profit surged 29% supported by concert attendance and merchandise sales.

GSK to Receive $370 Million in U.S. Patent Litigation Settlement

GSK said it would receive $370 million as part of a U.S. patent settlement between CureVac and BioNTech related to messenger mRNA-based Covid-19 vaccines.

The U.K. pharmaceutical giant said Friday that, under the terms of an existing license agreement with CureVac, it would receive an upfront payment of $320 million in cash, with the rest of the money through an amendment to the deal with CureVac. It will also receive a 1% royalty on future U.S. sales of influenza, Covid-19 and other related combination mRNA vaccine products made by BioNTech and Pfizer.

AI Spending Is Propping Up the Economy, Right? It's Complicated.

A billion dollars here, a billion dollars there. At some point we're going to be talking about real money.

We're already there when looking at the eye-watering tallies for artificial intelligence investment from the four biggest hyperscalers. Microsoft, Amazon, Facebook parent Meta Platforms, and Google parent Alphabet are likely to spend a staggering $340 billion this year alone in building out their AI data centers and developing their individual products.

In Latest Tariff Onslaught, China Isn't a Target. Here's Why.

It has been a big week for the Trump administration's tariffs, with the U.S. announcing 100% tariffs on semiconductor chips and additional 25% tariffs on India for its purchase of oil Wednesday and levies on 90 of its trading partners going into effect. But China, the biggest buyer of Russian oil and the country which has the widest trade deficit with the U.S., escaped the latest onslaught.

On Thursday, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick told Fox Business that President Donald Trump hasn't ruled anything out yet in terms of tariffs on other countries that buy Russian oil as he tries to turn up the heat on Russia's Vladimir Putin for his attack on Ukraine. Lutnick also echoed other administration officials, saying he expects the Aug. 12 deadline for tariffs on China to rise another 34% would be extended. However, he said the final decision rests with Trump, who hasn't yet announced an extension. The delay in extending the deadline has raised questions about whether the two sides have hit another speed bump in their complicated rivalry, potentially around export restrictions-on critical minerals access for the U.S. and on advanced technology for China.

The 30-Year Bond Auction Went Poorly. Yields Are Popping Higher.

An auction of government bonds that expires in three decades saw weak demand on Thursday, in an ominous sign for the Treasury market.

The Treasury Department sold $25 billion worth of 30-year debt at a yield of 4.813%, which was more than two basis points above the yield's level at the pre-bidding deadline. Higher rates at auctions signal that demand is weak, as the Treasury has to entice investors to buy U.S. debt. One basis point is equal to 0.01%.

Israeli Security Cabinet Approves Gaza Control Plan

TEL AVIV-Israel's security cabinet approved a plan for the military to take control of Gaza City after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu pledged to take over the entire enclave, a gamble that defies international pressure to end the war and lacks broad domestic support.

Netanyahu said Israel doesn't plan to hold on to Gaza and would transfer control of the territory to "Arab forces that will govern it properly." Israel would keep what he called a security perimeter around the enclave, he said in an interview with Fox News on Thursday.

Ukraine Repels a Russian Offensive, as Putin Seeks Trump Talks

SUMY, Ukraine-Ukrainian forces have halted Russia's summer offensive in the north of the country, recapturing some land and underlining Moscow's inability to put together decisive operations in its three-and-a-half-year war.

While Ukraine has largely stopped Moscow's move into Sumy province, in which Russian troops advanced as close as 12 miles from the regional capital, its own forces remain outgunned and might struggle to build on their momentum.

Trump Plans to Send Immigrant Detainees to Notorious Louisiana Prison

The Trump administration is planning to house immigrant detainees in a notorious Louisiana prison, according to people familiar with the matter, the latest effort to accommodate-and spotlight-its immigration crackdown.

The Louisiana State Penitentiary at Angola, once known as the bloodiest prison in the country, is the largest maximum security facility in the U.S. Spanning 18,000 acres sandwiched between the Mississippi River and the verdant Tunica Hills, it draws tourist groups and hosts prison rodeos where inmates compete. The Trump administration expects the facility will provide 450 beds to detained migrants and plans to announce it early next month, the people said.

Trump Orders Census to Exclude Immigrants Living in the U.S. Illegally

President Trump asked the Commerce Department to start working on a census that will exclude immigrants who entered the U.S. illegally.

"I have instructed our Department of Commerce to immediately begin work on a new and highly accurate CENSUS based on modern day facts and figures," he wrote in a social-media post Thursday morning. "People who are in our Country illegally WILL NOT BE COUNTED IN THE CENSUS."

FBI Will Help Track Down Democratic Texas Lawmakers, Cornyn Says

Sen. John Cornyn said the FBI will help track down Texas Democratic lawmakers who fled the state, blocking Republicans from taking up redistricting legislation.

Cornyn, a Texas Republican, didn't say what authority the Federal Bureau of Investigation had over the lawmakers, who haven't been accused of any crime. But he said FBI Director Kash Patel approved his request to have the agency assist state and local law enforcement in locating the Democratic legislators.

North Korean, Wrapped in Plastic Foam, Floats to Freedom in the South

SEOUL-A North Korean man defected to South Korea after he swam down the coast and evaded armed border guards with orders to shoot anyone fleeing Kim Jong Un's regime.

The unidentified man was detected late on the evening of July 30, according to South Korean officials, who made the defection public on Thursday. He used tied-on plastic foam for buoyancy and swam down the Korean Peninsula's west coast.

data.title: Landbay Inc SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Landbay Inc, a New York-based company incorporated in 2016, has released its latest Form 10-K report, detailing significant shifts in its business model and financial performance. Originally focused on trading securities and whisky, the company has transitioned to operating a furniture retail and design business, and has further expanded into the technology sector through its subsidiary Zhejiang Toumi Holding Co., Ltd. in China. This report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial highlights, business performance, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue – sales: $564,781. The increase in revenue was mainly due to the start of new operations in online marketing through its new subsidiary.
  • Gross profit: $56,611. Achieved from the sale of network equipment and providing online data marketing services.
  • Loss from operations: $(50,127). The increase in operating expenses was due to increased professional fees.
  • Net loss: $(53,100). The increase in net loss was mainly due to a decrease in nonrecurring disposal gain on fixed assets that occurred in 2024, and an increase in operating expenses.
  • Loss per share, basic and diluted: $(0.00). Reflects the net loss per share for the reporting period.

Business Highlights

  • Company Overview: Landbay Inc has shifted its business focus from trading securities and whisky to operating a furniture retail and design business in New York. The company has also expanded into the technology sector through its subsidiary Zhejiang Toumi Holding Co., Ltd., established in Hangzhou, China.
  • Change in Control: Significant changes in control occurred with Northern Ifurniture Inc acquiring a majority stake in 2019, followed by Chunyang Liu acquiring a controlling interest in 2024, leading to a complete overhaul of the management team.
  • Subsidiary Operations: Zhejiang Toumi, a wholly-owned subsidiary, focuses on technical consulting, technology development, software development, and electronic product sales. It generated net revenue of $564,781 for the year ended March 31, 2025, primarily from network equipment sales and online data marketing services.
  • Product Offerings: The company offers intelligent data-driven marketing services and customized technology development services. These services include programmatic advertising, content marketing, and the development of custom digital solutions like CRM, ERP, and BI systems.
  • Sales and Marketing Strategy: Landbay Inc employs a consultative sales approach, leveraging digital channels, industry events, and partner ecosystems to generate leads and enhance brand recognition. The company focuses on understanding client needs and tailoring solutions to meet those needs.
  • Competitive Landscape: The company operates in a competitive environment with both traditional digital marketing agencies and emerging data-tech platforms. Its competitive edge lies in integrating marketing strategy with advanced data technology to deliver measurable outcomes.
  • Regulatory Compliance: Landbay Inc complies with data privacy and cybersecurity regulations in China, including the Personal Information Protection Law and the Cybersecurity Law, ensuring that its data practices align with legal requirements.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to launch a live software service business through Zhejiang Toumi, although technical issues have delayed the initial launch scheduled for August 2024. The company is focused on improving business profitability and generating sufficient cash flow to meet operational needs.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Business Focus Shift: Landbay Inc has shifted its business focus from trading securities and whisky to operating a furniture retail and design business in New York, and further expanded into the technology sector by establishing a wholly owned subsidiary, Zhejiang Toumi Holding Co., Ltd., in China. This subsidiary is aimed at launching a live software service business, although technical issues have delayed its launch.
  • Acquisitions: The company acquired 100% ownership of Zhejiang Lanwan Biotechnology Co., Ltd. through its subsidiary, Zhejiang LGK.
  • Capital Management: The company has faced challenges with a working capital deficiency, which was $48,450 as of March 31, 2025, compared to $97,392 the previous year. This improvement was primarily due to loan forgiveness by related parties amounting to $102,395. The company has also relied on shareholder loans for working capital, with a total of $101,437 borrowed from the President and CEO. No equity repurchases or dividends were declared, and the company does not have any equity compensation plans.
  • Future Outlook: Management plans to alleviate the substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern by improving business profitability and generating sufficient cash flow from operations. They also aim to obtain additional working capital from the majority shareholder and the President to meet anticipated cash requirements. However, there is no assurance that these plans will be sufficient to fund ongoing capital expenditures and other requirements.

Challenges and Risks

  • Operational Risks: The company has undergone significant changes in its business focus, shifting from trading securities and whisky to operating a furniture retail and design business, and now to providing intelligent data-driven marketing services and customized technology development. This transition presents operational risks as the company adapts to new markets and business models. Additionally, the establishment of a wholly owned subsidiary, Zhejiang Toumi Holding Co., Ltd., in China introduces potential challenges related to managing operations across different regulatory environments.
  • Financial Challenges: The company faces financial challenges, including a working capital deficiency and an accumulated deficit, which raise substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern. The company has experienced negative financial trends, such as cash outflow from operating activities and operating losses. Management plans to address these issues by improving business profitability and securing additional working capital from the majority shareholder and the President of the company. However, there is no assurance that these plans will be sufficient to meet the company's ongoing capital requirements.
  • Technical Delays: The company also faces operational risks related to the delayed launch of its live software service business through its subsidiary, Zhejiang Toumi, due to technical issues. This delay could impact revenue generation and market positioning.

SEC Filing: Landbay Inc [ LNBY ] - 10-K - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: Amprius Technologies Is Maintained at Buy by HC Wainwright & Co.
Rice Futures at Near 1-Month High
data.description:
Rice futures rose to around $12.70 per hundredweight, their highest level since July 11, as traders considered the impact of ongoing global trade tensions.

President Donald Trump’s announcement of a 50% tariff on Indian rice imports, effective August 27, could significantly raise prices for U.S. consumers—especially for basmati rice, a key variety imported from India.

The latest USDA'S WASDE report indicated 2025/26 US rice outlook was lowered in July, with reduced supplies, domestic use, exports, and ending stocks.

Nevertheless, the supply outlook remains bullish, supported by India’s strong production and growing stockpiles.

India is processing record volumes of rice into ethanol to manage surplus grain, with government stocks rising 15% year-on-year to a record high in July.

data.title: India's Nifty, Sensex suffer longest weekly losing streak in five years on trade, earnings fears
data.description:
India's equity benchmarks fell on Friday, posting their sixth straight weekly loss as U.S. tariffs, trade uncertainty and muted earnings dampened sentiment.

The Nifty 50 and the BSE Sensex dropped 0.95% each to 24,363.3 points and 79,857.79, respectively. For the week, they shed 0.8% and 0.9%, respectively. This would be their longest losing streak since April 2020.

Losses were broad-based, with 13 of 16 major sectors ending the week in the red. Small-cap and mid-cap indexes declined 1.4% and 1.1%.

IT and pharma indexes lost 0.7% and 2.8%, while financials and energy fell 1.2% and 1.4%, respectively.

Investor sentiment remained fragile amid ongoing uncertainty over a potential U.S.-India trade deal and underwhelming earnings.

On Thursday, U.S. President Donald Trump ruled out further talks until the tariff dispute is resolved. This followed a move to double tariffs to 50%, citing India's oil imports from Russia.

"Prolonged tariff tensions and relentless foreign selling—totaling $3.47 billion since early July—have injected a fresh wave of volatility and caution into Indian markets," said Manish Goel, founder and MD at Equentis Wealth Advisory Services.

Among individual stocks, Adani Enterprises fell 7.4% this week, extending losses after posting disappointing quarterly results on July 31.

Textile exporters including KPR Mill , Gokaldas Exports , Vardhman Textiles , and Trident fell 4.2%-12.1% after the U.S. hiked duties on Indian imports.

In contrast, Hero MotoCorp surged 6.7% for the week, topping the Nifty 50 after a surprise earnings beat, aided by stronger exports.

On the day, LIC gained 3.2% on a quarterly profit rise.

Bharti Airtel dropped 3.3% on multiple block deals at a discount.

"Markets are also adjusting to the RBI's message that the economy should not expect too much incremental support from monetary policy measures for now," said Sandeep Bagla, CEO of TRUST Mutual Fund.

The RBI held rates steady on Wednesday but flagged tariff-related risks to the economy.

data.title: Expedia shares soar on upbeat forecast, US travel rebound
data.description:
Shares of Expedia surged more than 17% in premarket trading on Friday, after the online travel agent raised its full-year gross bookings forecast and struck an optimistic tone on the recovery in U.S. travel demand.

Expedia is the latest travel company to hint at a rebound in demand, following weakness earlier this year when consumers fretted over the economic impact of President Donald Trump's tariff policies.

"Since the beginning of July, we've seen an uptick in overall travel demand, particularly in the U.S.," CEO Ariane Gorin said on the earnings call on Thursday.

The company expects 2025 gross bookings to grow between 3% to 5%, up 1 percentage point from its earlier forecast.

Morningstar analyst Dan Wasiolek expects bookings growth to accelerate further to 7% in 2026 as demand improves alongside policy visibility.

Tariffs had disrupted travel spending, "but it appears prospective U.S. travelers are prepared to book again," said Danni Hewson, head of financial analysis at AJ Bell.

Expedia has also been focusing on simplifying its organizational structure by eliminating roles, streamlining operations and deploying generative AI technology.

Its second-quarter margin grew by 190 basis points, surpassing the company's May guidance of a 75- to 100-basis-point increase.

The biggest fundamental takeaway is that Expedia's continued strategic focus and tighter expense controls are driving more consistent results, said Baird analyst Michael Bellisario.

Expedia also joined industry peers Marriott and Airbnb in noting strong bookings from higher-income consumers while lower-income consumers were more cautious with discretionary spending.

Expedia's shares trade at about 12.01 times their forward profit estimates, below the industry median of 14.19.

data.title: Monster Beverage Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Monster Beverage Corp, a leading player in the energy drink industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and robust business performance, driven by strategic pricing actions, international market expansion, and new product launches.

Financial Highlights

Monster Beverage Corp reported impressive financial results for the three-month period ended June 30, 2025:

  • Net Sales: $2,111.6 million, reflecting an increase from $1,900.6 million in the same period last year, driven by price increases and international market expansion.
  • Gross Profit: $1,176.4 million, up from $1,019.5 million, benefiting from the Pricing Actions implemented in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Operating Income: $631.6 million, compared to $527.2 million in the prior year, indicating improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Income: $488.8 million, an increase from $425.4 million, supported by higher sales and effective cost management.
  • Net Income Per Common Share - Basic: $0.50, up from $0.41, reflecting the company's strong financial performance.
  • Net Income Per Common Share - Diluted: $0.50, compared to $0.41, demonstrating consistent earnings growth.

Business Highlights

In addition to its financial achievements, Monster Beverage Corp also reported notable business developments:

  • Revenue Segments: The Monster Energy® Drinks segment remains the largest contributor, representing 91.7% of net sales for the three-months ended June 30, 2025. The Strategic Brands segment accounted for 6.2% of net sales, while the Alcohol Brands segment contributed 1.8%, and the Other segment made up 0.3%.
  • Geographical Performance: Net sales to customers outside the United States increased by 15.8% to $864.2 million for the three-months ended June 30, 2025, accounting for approximately 41% of total net sales. This growth was driven by strong performance in international markets despite an unfavorable impact from foreign currency exchange rates.
  • New Product Launches: During the three-months ended June 30, 2025, the company introduced several new products, including Monster Energy® Lando Norris Zero Sugar, Monster Energy® Valentino Rossi Zero Sugar, and a range of Blind Lemon® flavored lemonades.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its growth strategy by further developing domestic markets and expanding its international business. This includes leveraging the recent product launches and pricing actions to enhance market presence and profitability.

SEC Filing: Monster Beverage Corp [ MNST ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: Fortinet, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Fortinet, Inc., a global leader in broad, integrated, and automated cybersecurity solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant growth in both financial and operational metrics, underscoring the company's robust performance and strategic advancements in the cybersecurity industry.

Financial Highlights

Fortinet, Inc. reported strong financial results for the second quarter and first half of 2025, showcasing substantial growth across key metrics:

  • Total Revenue: $1,630.0 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, an increase of 14% compared to the same period last year. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, total revenue was $3,169.7 million, also a 14% increase year-over-year.
  • Gross Profit: $1,315.1 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, reflecting a 13% increase from the same period last year. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, gross profit was $2,561.7 million, a 16% increase year-over-year.
  • Operating Income: $458.0 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, up 5% from the same period last year. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, operating income was $911.8 million, a 20% increase year-over-year.
  • Net Income: $440.1 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, an increase from $379.8 million in the same period last year. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, net income was $873.5 million, up from $679.1 million in the same period last year.
  • Net Income Per Share (Basic): $0.57 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $0.50 in the same period last year. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, net income per share (basic) was $1.14, compared to $0.89 in the same period last year.
  • Net Income Per Share (Diluted): $0.57 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $0.49 in the same period last year. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, net income per share (diluted) was $1.13, compared to $0.88 in the same period last year.

Business Highlights

Fortinet's business performance in the second quarter of 2025 was marked by significant achievements and strategic initiatives:

  • Revenue Segments: Fortinet's revenue is diversified across product lines, with significant contributions from secure networking, unified SASE, and AI-driven SecOps. The company experienced growth in secure networking hardware products and term licenses, contributing to the increase in product revenue.
  • Geographical Performance: The EMEA region contributed 41% of total revenue, showing an 18% increase compared to the previous year. The Americas region also contributed 41% of total revenue, with an 11% increase, while the APAC region contributed 18%, marking a 10% increase.
  • Sales Units: Fortinet's product revenue growth was driven by increased sales of hardware products and software licensing, particularly in secure networking hardware products.
  • New Product Launches: Fortinet continues to innovate with its FortiAI and FortiEndpoint solutions, enhancing security operations and endpoint protection. These products integrate advanced capabilities like endpoint detection and response, contributing to the company's competitive edge.
  • New Production Launches: Fortinet expanded its data center operations with new real estate acquisitions in Germany, Canada, the United States, and the Netherlands, enhancing its capacity for data center operations, office space, and warehouse operations.
  • Future Outlook: Fortinet expects continued growth in product and service revenue for the remainder of 2025, driven by secure networking, unified SASE, and AI-driven SecOps. The company anticipates further expansion in its data center footprint and PoPs deployment, supporting its growth strategy.

SEC Filing: Fortinet, Inc. [ FTNT ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: Community West Bancshares SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Community West Bancshares, a prominent player in the banking sector, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report reveals significant financial improvements and strategic business expansions, particularly following the acquisition of Community West. This article delves into the key financial and operational highlights from the report.

Financial Highlights

Total Interest Income: Community West Bancshares reported a total interest income of $46.025 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, marking an increase from $43.997 million in the same period in 2024. This growth was driven by higher interest and fees on loans.

Net Interest Income Before Provision for Credit Losses: The company achieved a net interest income before provision for credit losses of $33.304 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, up from $29.057 million in the same period in 2024, indicating improved interest income performance.

Net Income: Community West Bancshares reported a net income of $7.832 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to a net loss of $6.290 million in the same period in 2024, highlighting a significant turnaround in profitability.

Basic Earnings Per Share: The basic earnings per share stood at $0.41 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to a loss of $0.33 per share in the same period in 2024, reflecting improved earnings performance.

Diluted Earnings Per Share: Similarly, the diluted earnings per share was $0.41 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to a loss of $0.33 per share in the same period in 2024, consistent with basic earnings per share.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Community West Bancshares operates primarily in the banking sector, with revenue streams derived from interest and fees on loans, interest on deposits in other banks, and interest and dividends on investment securities. Non-interest income is generated from service charges, net realized losses on sales and calls of investment securities, and other income sources such as service fees and bank-owned life insurance.

Geographical Performance: The acquisition of Community West has expanded the company's footprint in Central California, particularly enhancing its presence in the Central Coast region. This acquisition has diversified the company's commercial banking business and improved its loan-to-deposit ratio.

Sales Units: The company reported a total of $2,397,873,000 in gross loans as of June 30, 2025, with significant contributions from commercial real estate - non-owner occupied, manufactured housing, and commercial real estate - owner occupied segments.

Future Outlook: Community West Bancshares is focused on leveraging the synergies from the acquisition of Community West to enhance operational efficiencies and expand its commercial banking offerings. The management is optimistic about the potential for increased revenue from the expanded geographical presence and diversified product offerings.

SEC Filing: Community West Bancshares [ CWBC ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: Allbirds, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Allbirds, Inc., a company known for its sustainable footwear and apparel, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights for the three and six months ended June 30, 2025. The company has faced several challenges, including international distributor transitions and retail store closures, which have impacted its financial results.

Financial Highlights

Net Revenue: Allbirds reported net revenue of $39.7 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $71.8 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025. This represents a decrease from the same periods in 2024, primarily due to international distributor transitions and retail store closures.

Gross Profit: The company achieved a gross profit of $16.2 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $30.6 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025. Gross margins were 40.7% and 42.6% respectively, indicating a decline from the previous year.

Loss from Operations: Allbirds reported a loss from operations of $(16.5) million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $(39.4) million for the six months ended June 30, 2025. This shows a reduction in operating losses compared to the same periods in 2024.

Net Loss: The net loss for the three months ended June 30, 2025, was $(15.5) million, and $(37.4) million for the six months ended June 30, 2025. This is an improvement compared to the net loss of $(19.1) million and $(46.5) million for the same periods in 2024.

Net Loss Per Share: The net loss per share was $(1.92) for the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $(4.64) for the six months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $(2.45) and $(5.96) for the same periods in 2024, indicating a decrease in loss per share.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Allbirds generates revenue primarily from sales of footwear and apparel products through direct digital channels and physical retail stores. The majority of sales are through direct channels, with a focus on core franchise products to drive consumer engagement.

Geographical Performance: The United States remains the primary market for Allbirds, contributing significantly to net revenue. International markets also play a role, though specific countries did not exceed 10% of net revenue individually. The company has transitioned operations in several international markets to distributor partners.

Sales Units: As of June 30, 2025, Allbirds operates 24 stores, focusing on optimizing the store fleet to drive customer acquisition. The store count has decreased due to strategic closures in the United States and transitions to distributor partners in international markets.

New Product Launches: The company continues to innovate with sustainable materials, focusing on core franchise products to increase closet share among existing customers. New product launches are aligned with Allbirds' commitment to sustainability and comfort.

Future Outlook: Allbirds plans to continue scaling its brand awareness and customer acquisition efforts, focusing on cost-effective strategies. The company aims to optimize its omni-channel strategy and store fleet while maintaining a commitment to sustainability and reducing its carbon footprint.

SEC Filing: Allbirds, Inc. [ BIRD ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025
